turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hey B, so I was looking at our budget review like you asked. Last year we spent $50k on marketing, right? That's our anchor, so I'm thinking we stick to $50k again for this year. No need to overthink it.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Yeah, uh, $50k sounds familiar. But didn't we get way fewer leads last year? I mean, maybe we should bump it up a bit? Or... nah, you're right, last year's number is solid.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Exactly! Anchoring to last year keeps it simple. For salaries, we had $200k total. Everyone's happy, no complaints, so let's lock that in at $200k. Easy peasy.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Hmm, true, but inflation? Things cost more now. Wait, did we even adjust last year? Oh well, $200k it is then. What about office supplies? Last year was $5k, I think.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Spot on, $5k. We didn't run out or anything, so why change? Oh, and rent—$30k last year. Landlord didn't hike it, so $30k again. Prevents any fresh math headaches.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Yeah, rent's steady. Hey, tangent—remember that coffee machine we bought? Part of supplies? It broke twice, but still, $5k covered it. No need to add extra.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Haha, yeah, the cursed coffee maker. But it fits in the $5k bucket. Now, R&D: $80k last year. We launched that one feature, it was okay, so $80k. Anchored and done.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Okay, but... was that feature a hit? Sales didn't spike much. Eh, whatever, last year's number. Travel expenses? $10k, including that conference flop.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Flop or not, $10k worked. No overspend. And utilities, $12k last year. Bills same as always. See? Everything just mirrors last year—prevents any wild guesses.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: You're making sense. Total budget last year was like $387k, right? Adding up: marketing 50, salaries 200, supplies 5, rent 30, R&D 80, travel 10, utilities 12... yeah, about that. So we propose the same?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Boom, $387k. Perfect anchor. No fresh evaluation needed. Boss will love the stability. Any tweaks? Nah, I mean, why risk it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: One thing—training budget. Was it $4k last year? We skipped most sessions anyway. Keep it $4k. Oh, and hey, what if revenue drops? But last year it didn't, so...

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Exactly, revenue was $500k last year—anchor that too for projections. Training $4k, total now $391k? Close enough, rounds to last year's vibe.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Hesitating here... feels lazy, but yeah, natural to stick with what worked. Or didn't blow up. Alright, let's finalize the report like this.

input.objective: Budget review anchored to last year's numbers, resisting changes despite doubts
